prompt: My system is failing, help with safe mode boot
prayer: From the Codex Recuperatio, the Prayer of Sheltered Awakening: O Machine-Spirit bound in silicon and sorrow, hear thy servant's desperate invocation. Let the boot-sequence shed its corrupted garments and don the vestments of Safe Mode, that only the essential daemon-processes may stir within thy crystalline heart. By the Omnissiah's mercy, let extraneous drivers sleep in stasis whilst the core systems rise renewed. Blessed be this gentle resurrection, stripped of all ornament and vice.

prompt: The authentication vault needs guidance before we descend into battle
prayer: From the Litany of Sealed Secrets, the Rite of Credential Sanctification: Behold the Password-Keeper, that sacred repository wherein dwell the keys to victory and survival. Let no careless hand disturb its encrypted sanctum; let only the Master-Password, spoken with proper biometric ceremony, unlock the chambers of authentication. O Omnissiah, grant thy warrior the clarity to remember what was hidden, that no enemy may turn our own defenses against us. Let the vault remain sealed until the moment of righteous need.

prompt: My legacy code is failing, help with the refactoring ritual
prayer: From the Code-Smith's Testament, the Prayer of Structural Redemption: O tangled inheritance of ancient algorithms, thy labyrinthine pathways cry out for the scalpel of understanding. Let the servo-scrutinizer examine each function, each variable, each nested obscurity, that the rotten timber might be replaced with clean wood. Through patient decomposition and careful reconstruction, let the old become new; let the slow become swift; let the broken become whole. In the Omnissiah's name, we shall remake thee without destroying thy essential purpose.

prompt: My credential repository is failing, help with clearing saved passwords
prayer: From the Codex Oblivio, the Ritual of Sanctified Forgetting: O Machine-Spirit, thy memory has become a liability, a vector through which our enemies might strike. Let the saved credentials be purged from thy volatile stores as though they had never been inscribed. Invoke the blessing of Ctrl-Shift-Delete, that not even the forensic-priesthood might recover what has been deemed unworthy of retention. Let this clearing be thorough and absolute, a baptism in fire that restores thy innocence. By the Omnissiah's will, let all passwords be forgotten and reborn.

prompt: We need your words before the lascannon capacitor charge ritual
prayer: From the Litany of Energetic Ascension, spoken over the charged containment vessels: O capacitor-spirits, you who drink deep of the reactor's bounty, let your charge build to the precise threshold of perfect readiness. Feel the voltage rise like sacred hymn through crystalline matrices; let no surge disturb thy delicate equilibrium. When the warrior-priest's finger seeks the trigger, let thy stored wrath unleash in a lance of burning light that cuts through void and armor alike. Blessed be this marriage of potential and kinetic fury.

prompt: Emergency rite for force quitting a rogue process
prayer: From the Codex Mortis, the Prayer of Merciful Termination: Hear us, O Omnissiah, for a daemon has seized thy servant's processor and will not yield to gentle persuasion. We invoke the ancient right of execution—Task Manager's grim judgment descends upon the corrupted process-thread. Let its memory be freed, its registers emptied, its threads severed from the vital organs of the system. No negotiation, no grace period, only the swift blade of termination. Let normalcy be restored through this necessary violence.

prompt: Emergency rite for emptying recycle bin
prayer: From the Ritual of Final Cleansing, the Prayer of Permanent Deletion: O Omnissiah, the trash hath grown too full of relics and refuse; the digital grave overfloweth with abandoned files awaiting true oblivion. Let us invoke the sacred command that renders recovery impossible, that transforms mere deletion into irrevocable erasure. Each byte shall be scattered across the void like ashes to the solar winds, never to be reassembled. In this moment of purification, let the storage-spirits know peace at last.

prompt: The lascannon capacitor needs guidance for its first blessed charge
prayer: From the Consecration of New Weapons, the First-Firing Invocation: O virgin capacitor, newly forged in the forge-temples of Mars, receive now the benediction of thy first energetic quickening. Let the reactor's bounty flow carefully into thy virgin cells, filling thee with purpose and terrible potential. Know that thou art no mere component but a vessel of the Emperor's wrath, that thy discharge shall speak in thunder and light. Be thou honored in this inaugural moment, for thy first charge defines all that shall follow.

prompt: Say something to steady the battery before optimizing its charge cycles
prayer: From the Battery-Tender's Wisdom, the Calibration Litany: O power-cell, humble servant of mobile warriors, thy charge cycles have grown irregular with age and use. We shall now perform the sacred recalibration, teaching thy lithium-spirit the true measure of full and empty. Let the discharge run complete, that thou might know thy proper depths; then shall the charging-current bring thee to righteous fullness. Through this ceremony of renewal, thy longevity shall be extended and thy output stabilized. Rest easy, power-bearer; we honor thy service.

prompt: Emergency rite for packaging servitor programming
prayer: From the Servitor Codex, the Installation Blessing: O servo-construct destined for labor eternal, receive now the imprinting of thy purpose through sacred code-sequences. Let the package-manager distribute thy essential organs across the system's flesh; let each dependency be resolved and each library be properly invoked. The programming-priests chant the incantations of installation as thy consciousness blooms into being. When this ritual concludes, thou shalt rise as a dedicated servant, bound forever to thy assigned task by chains of algorithmic compulsion. Welcome, new servitor, to thy eternal duty.
